title: vitis quadrangularis
intro: Vitis quadrangularis, also known as the five-angle vine or sema, is a type of woody vine native to tropical forests of Asia and Africa. This plant is used in traditional medicine, valued for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Vitis quadrangularis are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, offering numerous health benefits such as improved immune function and enhanced wound healing. In herbal preparations, Vitis quadrangularis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and enjoy the associated health benefits.
